name: Almond Cheese Horseshoe
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dough cycle and remove to a greased bowl. Rest in refrigerator for 30 minutes.
2. Knead briefly on a floured board and roll into a 15x10 inch rectangle.
3. Spread filling, leaving a half-inch border on all sides.
4. Starting along one long side, roll into a jellyroll and tuck and seal the ends.
5. Seal the long seam and place on a greased baking sheet, shaping into a horseshoe.
6. Cut slits with scissors through the top of the dough at two-inch intervals.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 45 minutes.
8. Brush the top with beaten egg or egg substitute and bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 15 to 20 minutes until golden.
9. Cool slightly and drizzle with powdered sugar glaze.
10. Almond Filling: Combine in a small bowl and mix to spreading consistency. Adjust sweetness and flavor as desired by adding more extract, sugar, or milk (if needed).
11. For a festive touch, add candied cherries in slits and sprinkle with sliced almonds before baking.
